detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
examples: Property foo is required but is missing.

errors.description: Optional list of individual error details

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. This value should not change between occurrences of the error.

type.description: A URI reference to human-readable documentation for the error.

examples: Production fabric used by production workloads.

post.summary: Create a new compute engine configuration

patch.description:
Applies the same roles-map entry (group + role) to each of the specified compute-engine-configs in parallel (up to 20 concurrent workers).

For each config ID the service:

1. Validates the config exists.
2. GETs the current resource from CIDS `/resources/:id`.
3. Merges the provided entry into the existing roles-map (replaces customer_groups if the role already exists, appends if new).
4. PUTs the full updated resource back to CIDS `/resources/:id`.
5. If the Compute Engine is in `RUNNING` state, pushes the updated groups to SCIM via `PUT /tdscim/v1/config/targets/:id`.

When the request is valid and reaches the processing stage the response is always `200 OK`. Individual failures are reported per config ID in the `errors` map without aborting updates for the other IDs. An empty (or absent) `errors` object means all updates succeeded. Invalid requests return `400`, unauthorized callers `403`, and infrastructure-level failures return `5xx`.

Only TD-Customer-Admin users are authorized to access this endpoint.

schema.description: Request payload for PATCH /configs/roles-map. Applies the same roles-map entry to each listed compute-engine-config.

compute_engine_config_ids.description: List of compute-engine-config IDs to update. Must contain between 1 and 1000 IDs.

roles-map.description: A single role-to-group mapping forwarded to the CIDS service. Matches the shape expected by CIDS PUT /resources/:id.

group.description: Customer group to associate with the role.

role.description: Role name to assign the group to.

requestBody.description: The roles-map entry to apply and the list of config IDs to update.

200.description: Bulk update completed. Check the errors field — if present it contains per-config error messages for any IDs that failed.

put.description: Replaces the entire roles-map (role-to-customer-group mappings) for a specific compute-engine-config. This uses PUT semantics — the existing roles-map is fully replaced by the provided payload. All other CIDS resource fields (customer_erp, site, resource_type, service_name, description) are preserved by fetching the current record from CIDS before issuing the PUT.

patch.description:
Updates settings for a specific site. Supports two phases:

Phase 1 (CE not yet SUCCESS): only compute_engine_setup.enabled=true is accepted. Initiates Compute Engine setup (network provisioning via GNS).

Phase 2 (CE SUCCESS): allows updating default_deploy_type, pooled_network_settings, and dedicated_network_settings.

schema.description: Request body for patching site settings. Phase 1 (before CE SUCCESS): only compute_engine_setup.enabled=true is accepted. Phase 2 (after CE SUCCESS): default_deploy_type, pooled_network_settings, and dedicated_network_settings may be updated.

compute_engine_setup.description: Patchable portion of CE setup — only enabled=true is supported in Phase 1

enabled.description: Set to true to initiate Compute Engine setup

allowed_accounts.description: Allowed AWS account IDs for Private Link
Allowed AWS account IDs (required when connectivity_type is privatelink)

allowed_cidrs.description: Allowed CIDR ranges (required when connectivity_type is internet)

items.description: An allowed CIDR entry

schema.description: Response for site settings PATCH operation

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when CE setup completed

enabled.description: Whether Compute Engine setup has been initiated

initiated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when CE setup was first triggered

steps.description: Ordered list of setup steps and their individual statuses

items.description: Status of an individual CE setup step

200.description: CE setup already complete (idempotent enable) or Phase 2 settings updated
